This is a minimal, reproducible demo extracted from [workbook.qmd](workbook.qmd) to generate the Seville interactive flow map.

```{r}
pak::pak("tdscience/tartu26")
library(flowmapblue)
library(tidyverse)
library(htmlwidgets)
```

```{r}
#| label: flowmap-seville-demo
#| echo: false
url_flows <- "https://github.com/tdscience/tartu26/releases/download/v1/flows_seville_time.csv"
flows_seville_time <- read_csv(url_flows)
url_locations <- "https://github.com/tdscience/tartu26/releases/download/v1/locations_seville.csv"
locations_seville <- read_csv(url_locations)
flowmap_seville_interactive <- flowmapblue(
  locations = locations_seville,
  flows = flows_seville_time,
  mapboxAccessToken = Sys.getenv("MAPBOX_TOKEN"),
  darkMode = TRUE,
  animation = FALSE,
  clustering = TRUE
)
# 'Print' the object to render the widget directly:
# flowmap_seville_interactive

# Save a standalone widget file and embed it via iframe to isolate styles/scripts.
htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
  flowmap_seville_interactive,
  file = "docs/seville_flowmap_embed.html",
  selfcontained = TRUE
)
```

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="seville_flowmap_embed.html"
  title="Interactive flow map of Seville"
  style="width: 100%; height: 760px; border: 1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius: 8px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>
```

<details>
<summary>Getting a Mapbox token</summary>

Get a free token from https://account.mapbox.com/access-tokens/

``` r
Sys.setenv(MAPBOX_TOKEN = "YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN")
```
</details>
